title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Ear Infection
intro: Herbal teas for ear infection are a natural remedy that involves brewing and drinking teas made from various herbs to alleviate symptoms and promote healing of the ear. These teas can be effective in treating ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain, swelling, and infection. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat ear infections include ginger tea, which reduces inflammation and soothes the ear; eucalyptus tea, which decongests the ear and promotes drainage; peppermint tea, which cools and calms the ear; garlic tea, which has antimicrobial properties that combat infection; and licorice root tea, which soothes and protects the ear. By incorporating these herbal teas into a treatment plan, individuals can potentially alleviate their ear infection symptoms and promote a faster recovery.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and decongestant properties. This herbal preparation helps to treat ear infections by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and clearing excess mucus from the ear, thus providing relief from symptoms such as fever, headache, and discomfort. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus globulus teas, including eucalyptol, limonene, and alpha-pinene, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause ear infections. The benefits of using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat ear infections include natural and non-invasive relief, reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, and fewer side effects compared to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the leaves from the liquid.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, to make it sweeter.
Use the tea as ear drops by pouring a small amount into the affected ear, tilting your head to let it drain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be effective in treating ear infections, but they may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, or interactions with other medications. When using Eucalyptus globulus teas to treat ear infections, precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which can lead to nausea, dizziness, or an imbalance of essential oils in the body.
preparation_usage: Warm the affected ear with a towel for 5 minutes.
Soak 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus in 1 cup of hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the solids, then let it cool slightly.
Gently pour 5-7 drops of the cooled liquid into the affected ear, twice daily.
Repeat for 3-5 days, or until the infection subsides, under adult supervision.

preparation_amazon.title: Jovvily Eucalyptus Leaves - 1lb - Dried - Cut & Sifted - Herbal Tea

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections in the ear. The tea's ability to stimulate the immune system and increase the production of white blood cells also aids in fighting off the infection. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat ear infections include reduced pain, swelling, and discharge, as well as a faster recovery time, making it a popular herbal remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the Echinacea flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, for ear infection relief.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can cause side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate in some individuals when used to treat ear infections. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take precautions by starting with a low dose, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding use if you have allergies or are pregnant, and also avoid using it for extended periods.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Use an ear dropper to put 2-3 drops into the infected ear.
Repeat the process 2-3 times a day for 5-7 days.
Combine with other treatments as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents, including gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial growth in the ear canal, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain and discomfort. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, these compounds promote healing and reduce the risk of complications associated with ear infections. Regular consumption of Zingiber officinale teas may provide relief from ear infection symptoms and promote overall ear health, making it a potential natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried ginger root, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils.
Add the dried ginger root to the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the ginger root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink it while warm, up to 3 times a day for ear infection relief.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Mycoses", Zingiber officinale teas for ear infection may not be directly studied or recommended, but ginger extracts from Zingiber officinale exhibited pronounced antifungal activity, suggesting potential use in preventing fungal infections, including ear infections caused by fungi.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat ear infections due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals. To use Zingiber officinale teas safely, it is essential to start with small doses, avoid mixing it with other medications, and monitor your body's reaction to it, especially if you have allergies or sensitive stomach.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor for ear infection diagnosis and approval to use Zingiber officinale tea.
Gently heat 1 cup of water to boil, then steep 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale for 5 minutes.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3 times a day, with a gap of 6 hours between each intake.
Use Zingiber officinale tea for 7-10 days continuously, as directed by a doctor.
Monitor ear infection symptoms and report any changes to a doctor for further guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause ear infections. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory and antioxidant effects that aid in the healing process of ear infections. The anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea angustifolia teas help to reduce pain and swelling in the ear, while its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of pathogens, thereby treating the underlying cause of the infection. The benefits of using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat ear infections include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, fewer side effects, and a potential for faster recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea angustifolia roots. You can buy them at a health store.
Measure 2 tablespoons of dried roots and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Boil 1 cup of water and pour it over the Echinacea roots. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the roots. Let it cool down to a comfortable temperature.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve ear infection symptoms. Consult a doctor before using it.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, as well as potential exacerbation of underlying conditions such as kidney or liver disease. Precautions include avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women, and individuals with weakened immune systems, as well as taking the tea in moderation and monitoring for any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1/2 cup of Echinacea angustifolia tea 3 times a day
Take 2 tablespoons of Echinacea angustifolia tea in the morning
Take 1 tablespoon of Echinacea angustifolia tea in the evening
Continue using tea for 7-10 days to help ear infection
Monitor ear infection and adjust dosage as needed with doctor's advice

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in these teas, particularly bilobalide and ginkgolides, help to reduce inflammation and combat the bacterial or fungal infections causing the ear infection. The antiseptic properties of Ginkgo biloba teas also aid in preventing the spread of infection and promoting a healthy environment for healing. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas may help alleviate ear infection symptoms, reduce the risk of complications, and promote overall ear health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Place the Ginkgo biloba leaves in the tea infuser or strainer.
Pour the boiling water over the leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, and consider consulting a doctor for ear infection treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects may include headaches, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its ability to thin the blood and interact with certain medications. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat ear infections, it is essential to take precautions, such as avoiding taking it if you have bleeding disorders, taking anticoagulant medications, or planning to undergo surgery, and also being cautious not to consume it in excess to minimize potential side effects.
preparation_usage: Consult doctor before using Ginkgo biloba for ear infection
Use 2-3 teaspoons of Ginkgo biloba tea in a cup
Drink 1 cup of tea 3 times a day
Soak a cotton ball in tea and apply to affected ear
Use for 5-7 days or as directed by doctor

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, also known as licorice root tea, have been used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and glycyrrhizin, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial and fungal infections in the ear. By reducing inflammation and killing off the underlying infection, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help to alleviate symptoms such as pain, discomfort, and hearing loss associated with ear infections. The benefits of using this herbal preparation to treat ear infections include its natural and non-invasive approach, which can be a welcome alternative to antibiotics and other conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste (optional).
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from ear infection symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Journal of the Medical Association of Thailand = Chotmaihet thangphaet", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas exhibited strong antimicrobial activity against S. pyogenes, S. pneumoniae, and S. mutans, which are commonly causing acute pharyngitis, and may potentially be used as an alternative to antibiotics.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure and potassium depletion due to its licorice root content, which can lead to an imbalance of electrolytes in the body. To minimize these risks, it's essential to consume these teas in moderation and maintain a balanced diet, also considering that excessive consumption can cause symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and fatigue.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball with 2-3 drops of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea.
Insert the cotton ball into the infected ear canal.
Leave it in place for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the cotton ball and discard it.
Repeat twice a day for 3-5 days or as directed by a doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the underlying infection and reduce inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents present in Cinchona officinalis teas, such as quinine and alkaloids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause ear infections. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be beneficial in treating ear infections due to its antimicrobial properties, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, nausea, and dizziness in some individuals. preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections in the ear. By reducing swelling and fighting off infection, Salvia officinalis teas can help to alleviate symptoms such as pain, fever, and hearing loss associated with ear infections. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, diarrhea, and stomach upset due to the high content of thujone, a compound that can be toxic in large amounts. To use Salvia officinalis teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small dosages, avoiding prolonged use, and monitoring your body's reaction, as excessive consumption may lead to adverse effects, particularly when used for extended periods or in individuals with sensitive stomachs.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 2-3 times a day.
Start with 1 tablespoon of dried leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes before straining and drinking.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can help alleviate ear infections, but it may cause potential side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased heart rate due to its active compounds like thymol and carvacrol. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Mentha x piperita teas can be used to treat ear infections, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects include stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high berberine content, as well as allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching. Precautions should be taken when using Hydrastis canadensis teas, such as starting with low doses and gradually increasing, avoiding it if pregnant or breastfeeding, and monitoring blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents present in yarrow tea, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections, thereby alleviating symptoms of ear infections. Drinking yarrow tea has been found to help in reducing ear pain, fever, and swelling associated with ear infections, while also promoting wound healing and preventing further complications. The benefits of using yarrow tea to treat ear infections include its ease of preparation, affordability, and minimal side effects, making it a popular natural remedy among individuals seeking a holistic approach to healthcare.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. If using dried, you can skip to step 3.
Add 1 tablespoon of Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers to 1 cup of boiling water. Let it boil for 5-7 minutes.
If using dried leaves and flowers, add 2 tablespoons to 1 cup of boiling water. Let it boil for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool. You can add a sweetener like honey if desired.
Drink 1/2 to 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to help alleviate ear infection symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Medicines (Basel, Switzerland), Achillea millefolium teas for ear infection are not specifically mentioned in the study, as the study focuses on the traditional uses, phytochemical constituents, and biological activities of Cherokee medicinal plants, but it does mention yarrow (Achillea millefolium) as one of the several Cherokee medicinal plants that are still in use today as herbal medicines.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications that may worsen the condition or exacerbate the infection. Precautions to take when using Achillea millefolium teas for ear infections include not consuming excessive amounts, monitoring for signs of worsening symptoms, and avoiding use in children or individuals with compromised immune systems.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, including flavonoids and terpenoids, exhibit their therapeutic effects by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and reducing oxidative stress. By using Silybum marianum teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and non-invasive treatment option that promotes the healing of ear infections without the risk of side effects associated with conventional medications. The benefits of this herbal preparation include its ability to alleviate symptoms such as fever, ear pain, and discharge, ultimately leading to the resolution of the infection.
preparation_procedure: Get Silybum marianum seeds or dried flowers. Use 1 tablespoon for every cup of boiling water.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and add 1 tablespoon of Silybum marianum seeds or dried flowers.
Let it steep for 5-10 minutes. Strain the mixture into a cup.
Add honey to taste, if desired. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day.
Consult a doctor before using Silybum marianum tea for ear infection. Use this tea in combination with other treatments.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided study, Silybum marianum teas cannot be determined as the herbs studied in the research are Saussurea laniceps, Saussurea involucrata, and Saussurea medusa, not Silybum marianum.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used as a potential treatment for ear infections, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and increased risk of bleeding due to its anti-coagulant properties. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation to treat ear infections, including monitoring for signs of allergic reactions, avoiding use in individuals with bleeding disorders, and being cautious when consuming it in large quantities or for extended periods.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas, derived from the wild thyme plant, have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to its antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat the underlying bacterial or fungal infection, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain associated with ear infections. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum teas, including thymol and carvacrol, exhibit antimicrobial activity that helps to eliminate the infection-causing pathogens from the ear canal. By using Thymus serpyllum teas, individuals can benefit from its natural and non-invasive approach to treating ear infections, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the leaves in a teapot.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Mix 1 teaspoon of honey in the tea to make it sweeter.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea throughout the day to help soothe the ear infection.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or itching, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea and stomach upset. To use Thymus serpyllum teas safely, it's recommended to start with small doses, avoid using it during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and be cautious when consuming it with other medications, as it may interact with them.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat ear infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The presence of bioactive constituents such as apigenin, luteolin, and chamazulene in these teas helps to reduce inflammation, combat bacterial and fungal infections, and alleviate pain. By treating the underlying cause of ear infections, Matricaria chamomilla teas can help to promote healing, reduce the risk of complications, and alleviate symptoms such as fever and discomfort. The benefits of using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat ear infections include a natural and non-invasive approach, reduced risk of side effects, and the potential for long-term relief from recurring infections.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 2 tbsp of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers, and a strainer.
Pour boiling water over the dried flowers in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a strainer.
Use the tea to fill the ear canal affected by infection, or apply a warm compress soaked in the tea.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Brazilian journal of otorhinolaryngology, Matricaria chamomilla teas for ear infection may be effective as the essential oil from Matricaria chamomilla was active against 3 strains of S. aureus and the Candida strains.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat ear infections, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as skin rashes or increased sensitivity to light, as well as stomach upset or diarrhea due to the tea's potential to interact with other medications or exacerbate underlying health conditions. Precautions when using Matricaria chamomilla teas to treat ear infections include avoiding consumption by pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with severe allergies or sensitivities, and monitoring for any adverse reactions, especially if taken in excess or combined with other herbal remedies.

preparation_study: According to Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine : eCAM, there is no direct evidence that Eucalyptus globulus teas are specifically used to treat ear infections in the provided study, however, Eucalyptus globulus is one of the top-cited plant species used in the treatment of respiratory tract infections (RTIs), which may include infections that can affect the ear such as middle ear infections, in Ethiopia.
intro_study: According to "Brazilian journal of otorhinolaryngology", teas for ear infection may be beneficial in treating outer ear infections, as some plant extracts such as Cymbopogon citratus and Matricaria chamomila essential oils showed in vitro antimicrobial activity against Staphylococcus aureus and Candida strains.
